transcript.text: There was some slight increase in terms of sessions and views, but nothing significant.
We just included a few links over here that we figured would be useful.
We're you're tracking these already, but using GSC data, basically, we just pulled together some pages that lost a bunch of clicks.
That's all of these URLs over here.
Some of these are the best credit cards.
You know, they were in that cluster, so not sure if we want to, you know, sort of update that page or maybe look at the cluster, but generally just including it for your information.
Some other criteria as well, generally, you know, lost a lot of positions combined with click and impression loss.
Like this page, for example, was on the cusp of top 10 three months ago, but it's completely fallen out.
And then, you know, generally, CTI, there were a lot of CTI drops, as expected, as AI overviews has rolled out.
We don't think it's much of a concern for these things.
So, okay, so just dropping that over there, and then, of course, these pages were generally on the bottom tier in terms of performance.
So, just listing that.
In terms of per diem, again, we saw a rise in views and sessions, but nothing major over there.
And again, quarterly performance, we just dropped a couple pages, best and worst performer.
Don't expect to update these pages, but just listing it in case.
With the expense category, this past week, we saw a marginal rise, but again, as we can see from the chart, there has been a steady decline.
The numbers aren't pretty on a quarterly basis, as the chart shows.
Most pages have lost more than 50% of their clicks.
Generally, the conclusion here is it's an algorithm update that really we can't do much about at this point.
Regarding tuning pages, we did see a drop in session.
But not a major drop in absolute terms.
On a quarterly basis, all pages did see a rise in clicks.
The one page that is a bit problematic this past three months has been the net present value block.
It kind of performed well initially when we refreshed it, but after that, it just started falling off.
My guess is it's competing pretty heavily with the likes of Investopedia, etc., which is a far more core topic for them.
And not so much for RAMP, which is why it kind of fell off.
The second batch, you know, it kind of fell to just one session this week, which is pretty poor, but there's not a lot of pages in this particular batch.
Generally, on a quarterly basis, these pages performed pretty well.
Just the how to improve cash flow didn't quite perform as well.
But for these treasury pages, they're really kind of, they're performing.
A lot better than I expected.
So just for more context, when we first proposed this, the idea was to have this as part of a broader programmatic sort of campaigns to coincide with the launch of Transparency.
But then we whittled it down quite a bit because there was the need to focus only on keywords that were already ranking.
We still snuck in a few pages in there.
For example, the liquidity position report, which doesn't have any tangible volume on SEMrush, but it's been the best performer for us.
And generally for all of these articles, there haven't been massive key volumes in SEMrush, but we still are getting some sessions and clicks from them.
So I just wanted to throw it out there.
Like if there's any idea for like a new programmatic cluster, this might be a good potential one.
So yeah, just dropping it in here for consideration.
The vendor pages generally, they've been pretty steady.

In terms of performance, we're not seeing a massive rise in terms of sessions or views, given the publishing, but generally it's, we feel it's lagging a bit in terms of performance, but we do want to wait and see how the next batch does and see whether it starts compounding at this point, which is, should start doing so.
Any questions about any of that?
All right.
So, generally, just some updates on what we're working on.
We're working on the publishing automation.
I think the team was looking at the alternatives pages, and we should have an update soon, early next week.
Regarding vendor pages, Victoria, this was created this morning, so it's not fully up to date for your latest comments.
We will review the pages, the edits on them, and start publishing.
Yeah.
I'll me something Thank Q I hope
Julie, Matt, I think you tagged us on the content refreshes as well.
So I think we're good to go over there.
I think the only outstanding item was the HTML JSON access in the CMS editing, Luke.
Not a big deal, but just wanted to point that out.
Yeah, our web engineering team is working on it.
And it's not, yeah, it's still broken.
Yeah.
So, yeah, ideally, I keep reiterating, like, the ideal state that we are able to just drop in JSON or HTML.
I don't know, I don't really understand why that's not happening.
But, yeah, like, we're basically blocked from even creating tables right now.
So you are more blocked.
Everyone's blocked.
Terrible.
So, yeah.
Oh, good.
No news is good news, I guess.
So, yeah, that's pretty much it for the update this week.
We'll get on publishing the vendor pages, and we'll update you, Victoria, with the deadlines for that.
Sounds good.
And I also added more for production to more categories.
Just add it now.
Yep.
We will schedule that, and we'll get going on those.
Sounds good.
Thank you.
All right.
Thanks, everyone.
Sorry, could I?
Yeah.
One question.
Yeah.
So for those tuning pages that you all had been working on the last few weeks, do you need anything else for me on those before we can update the pages in Sanity?
You mean the blog refreshes?
Yes.
Sorry.
Yeah.
If you feel they're good to publish that's pretty much it.
Do you want us to publish those?
Usually what we do is we just flag those as a status, good add to CMS or something, and then it's our key to the link.
Oh, okay.
So it would be helpful if I did that when I'm done with the review.
Yeah, so I can just show you real quick in the over here.
So basically there's a status add to CMS.
You know, if it's good to go, you can just flag it here and that's pretty much it.
So yeah.
Okay, cool.
And yeah, we could probably take this offline too, but if there were any other points that you wanted to put on around like selecting the author, selecting the publishing team, Insanity 2.
I think, Luke, we can talk about this offline too, but I raised a couple times whether we want to add ProText as a publishing team.
So we can like split out reporting if we want to.
We can do that.
Okay.
You don't need to add the value.
You don't need to.
Yeah, I don't think I need to.
Okay, cool.
All right, nice.
And then, yeah, my only other thing was for the tunes.
I'm sorry.
You're calling them the blog refreshes.
For those, we can maintain the existing author in there.
If there is one selected.
So that shouldn't be an issue with it.
But yeah, I'll update the stats for those in the sheet.
Okay, sounds good.
Yeah, we will schedule those as well.
It shouldn't be.
I don't think there's too many tables in those from what I remember.
So it shouldn't be too much of a look.
Yeah, we shipped some changes on the table today that like totally bricked it entirely.
So we rolled back to.
The old component, which is still broken, but at least we know that it's broken and we know what to look for, so, yeah.
Yeah, okay.
Okay.
Well, we'll deal with it as it comes, I guess.
Yeah.
So, okay, then.
Yeah, I will update everybody.
Nika will as well, with regards to the publishing and scheduling.
So, yeah.
Thanks, everyone.
All right.
Thank you.
Bye.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly check-in to discuss SEO performance, content updates, and ongoing projects for Ramp and GrowthX collaboration.](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Slight increases in sessions and views across various content categories, but no significant changes](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=4.0)
  - [Ongoing issues with HTML/JSON access in CMS editing, blocking table creation](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=307.0)
  - [New vendor pages and tuning pages showing steady performance, with some unexpected successes](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=236.0)
  - [Publishing automation and content refreshes are in progress, with new categories added for production](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=273.0)

## Topics

### SEO Performance Overview

  - [Slight increase in sessions and views across categories (credit cards, per diem, expense)](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=74.0)
  - [GSC data revealed pages with significant click losses, some falling out of top 10 rankings](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=44.0)
  - [CTR drops observed, likely due to AI overviews rollout (not considered a major concern)](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=49.0)
  - [Expense category showing steady decline, with most pages losing \>50% clicks quarterly](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=90.0)
  - [Algorithm update impact suspected, limited actionable steps available](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=109.0)

### Tuning Pages Performance

  - [Minor drop in sessions, but not significant in absolute terms](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=116.0)
  - [Quarterly basis shows increased clicks for most pages](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=124.0)
  - [Net Present Value block underperforming, possibly due to competition with finance-focused sites](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=129.0)
  - [Treasury pages performing better than expected, despite low SEMrush volumes](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)
  - [Potential for new programmatic cluster based on treasury content success](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=224.0)

### Vendor Pages Update

  - [Steady performance without massive increases in sessions or views](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=236.0)
  - [Performance lagging expectations, but team waiting to see if next batch compounds results](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)

### Ongoing Projects and Tasks

  - [Publishing automation in progress, update expected early next week](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=273.0)
  - [Alternatives pages being reviewed by the team](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=276.0)
  - [Content refreshes approved and ready for implementation](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [HTML/JSON access in CMS editing still problematic, blocking table creation](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)
  - [New categories added for production by Victoria](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)

### Content Publishing Process

  - [Matt to flag content as "add to CMS" when review is complete](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=424.0)
  - [Discussion on selecting authors and publishing teams in Sanity](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=457.0)
  - [Existing authors to be maintained for blog refreshes](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=501.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Review and start publishing vendor pages, with deadlines to be communicated to Victoria](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=367.0)
  - [Schedule and begin work on newly added production categories](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=385.0)
  - [Vivek/Nika to update team on publishing and scheduling progress](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=554.0)
  - [Matt to update status for tuning pages/blog refreshes in the sheet](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=513.0)
  - [Continue monitoring performance of vendor and tuning pages](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=236.0)
  - [Web engineering team to address ongoing CMS editing issues, particularly table creation](https://fathom.video/share/5ReTQwVguvwbsW35VQZwbJ7yf8rkMqN9?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)

action_items.description: Start publishing vendor pages; update Victoria w/ deadlines
Schedule production for new categories Victoria added
Update status of blog refreshes to "add to CMS" in sheet when ready
Schedule blog refreshes once Matt marks them ready
